title: Apply: evolutionary trees
description: Apply your understanding of evolutionary trees in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
The evolutionary tree below shows the relationships between three groups of organisms: fish, amphibians, and reptiles.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which part of the evolutionary tree represents the common ancestor of fish, amphibians, and reptiles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled reptiles at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled amphibians. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled fish. The point where the longer line branches off from the main line is labeled A. The point where the shorter line branches off from the main line is labeled B. A point on the main line between point B and the top is labeled C.

choices.content: the branch point labeled A
the branch point labeled B
the part of the branch labeled C

assessment_items.question_content:
The evolutionary tree below shows the relationships between three groups of organisms: fish, amphibians, and reptiles.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is represented by the circled branch point in the evolutionary tree?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled reptiles at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled amphibians. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled fish. The point where the shorter line branches off from the main line is circled.

choices.content: the common ancestor of fish and amphibians, but not reptiles
the common ancestor of amphibians and reptiles, but not fish
the common ancestor of fish, amphibians, and reptiles

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which diagram correctly shows how time is represented in an evolutionary tree?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: ![ A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right, leading to a picture of a chimpanzee. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and leads to a picture of a rabbit. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and leads to a picture of a turkey. The word past is in the bottom right corner of the diagram. The word present is in the top right corner of the diagram. An arrow points upwards from the word past to the word present.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d75b4f21fc908c9a4f104ad349905ec.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right, leading to a picture of a chimpanzee. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and leads to a picture of a rabbit. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and leads to a picture of a turkey. The word past is in the top right corner of the diagram. The word present is in the bottom right corner of the diagram. An arrow points downwards from the word past to the word present.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0938cb65e7d88c6d3c2aecf2b2726063.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right, leading to a picture of a chimpanzee. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and leads to a picture of a rabbit. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and leads to a picture of a turkey. The word past is in the bottom left corner of the diagram. The word present is in the bottom right corner of the diagram. An arrow points to the right from the word past to the word present.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/043208d53aac8aeb15f9d10519316ef9.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
The following evolutionary tree shows the relationships between species A, B, and C.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the evolutionary tree, which two species are most closely related?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Species that share a common ancestor in the more *recent* past are *more* closely related. Species that share a common ancestor in the more *distant* past are *less* closely related.

For the style of tree shown, a common ancestor that is closer to the top of the evolutionary tree is from the more recent past. A common ancestor closer to the bottom of the evolutionary tree is from the more distant past.

So, species B and C share a common ancestor in the more recent past. Species A and B, and species A and C, share a common ancestor in the more distant past.

[[☃ image 1]]
The two species that are most closely related are species B and species C.

options.alt: A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled species C at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled species B. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled species A.

choices.content: species A and species B
species A and species C
species B and species C

assessment_items.question_content:
The following evolutionary tree shows the relationships between species X, Y, and Z.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the evolutionary tree, which two species are most closely related?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Species that share a common ancestor in the more *recent* past are *more* closely related. Species that share a common ancestor in the more *distant* past are *less* closely related.

For the style of tree shown, a common ancestor that is closer to the top of the evolutionary tree is from the more recent past. A common ancestor closer to the bottom of the evolutionary tree is from the more distant past.

So, species Y and Z share a common ancestor in the more recent past. Species X and Y, and species X and Z, share a common ancestor in the more distant past.

[[☃ image 1]]
The two species that are most closely related are species Y and species Z.

options.alt: A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled species Z at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled species Y. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled species X.

choices.content: species X and species Y
species X and species Z
species Y and species Z

assessment_items.question_content:
The following evolutionary tree shows the relationships between species 1, 2, and 3.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the evolutionary tree, which two species are most closely related?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Species that share a common ancestor in the more *recent* past are *more* closely related. Species that share a common ancestor in the more *distant* past are *less* closely related.

For the style of tree shown, a common ancestor that is closer to the top of the evolutionary tree is from the more recent past. A common ancestor closer to the bottom of the evolutionary tree is from the more distant past.

So, species 2 and 3 share a common ancestor in the more recent past. Species 1 and 2, and species 1 and 3, share a common ancestor in the more distant past.

[[☃ image 1]]
The two species that are most closely related are species 2 and species 3.

options.alt: A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled species 3 at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled species 2. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled species 1.

choices.content: species 1 and species 2
species 1 and species 3
species 2 and species 3
